message:
Add runtime-compatible schema repair and harden table rebuild safety

Introduce apply_runtime_compatible_schema() as a lightweight repair path
that applies schema SQL, runs migrations, sets runtime PRAGMAs, and bumps
user_version — without the heavy lifting of full table rebuilds. This is
useful when the DB structure is fundamentally sound but may be missing
indices or have stale PRAGMAs.

Additional safety improvements to table rebuild logic:
- Add index_exists() helper to check for index presence before creation
- Add ISSUES_CLOSED_AT_CHECK constraint to rebuilt tables
- Rebuild now projects only existing columns into the new table, avoiding
  INSERT of NULL for columns that don't exist in the source table
- Error explicitly when no canonical issue columns are found during rebuild
  rather than silently producing an empty table
- Update test: legacy KV primary keys now correctly route through the full
  repair path instead of being treated as runtime-compatible

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
